name: lead-cannot-edit-files
description: When overstory lead hooks block file modification (Write/Edit/Bash edit commands), config-flip and integration tasks (e.g. biome.json rule severity change after cleanup) must be delegated to a dedicated builder — the lead cannot apply even trivial edits. Lead's role is pure orchestration.

name: parallel-lint-cleanup-decomposition
description: For single-rule mechanical lint cleanup spanning 400+ warnings across src/ and test/, split into 3 builders with non-overlapping file scope: one for src/ (production code, careful narrowing), two for test/ (split by warning count to balance load). Base all builders off the lead branch so their branches merge cleanly. File scopes must be truly disjoint — no directory globs that might overlap.

Banning 'as' casts blanket-wide in quality specs for test cleanup is unrealistic. When Biome's noNonNullAssertion ('!' forbidden) combines with noNonNullAssertedOptionalChain ('?.foo!' forbidden), the remaining narrowing options for tests that just inserted known-good records become verbose (if-guard + throw). Builders reasonably choose 'as' as next-best.
resolution: For future mechanical lint cleanups, scope the 'as' ban to production code only, or omit it from test-file specs. Document that 'as' is acceptable in tests where the record's existence is guaranteed by the test setup immediately above.
